problem_text: Evaluate the indefinite integral  integral 2x(x^2 + 1)^5 dx.

content.label: Recognize a reverse-chain-rule pattern
content.applies_when: the integrand contains an inner function g(x) = x^2 + 1 whose derivative 2x is already present as a factor

content.label: u-substitution (reverse chain rule)
content.symbolic: integral f(g(x)) g'(x) dx = integral f(u) du  (u = g(x), du = g'(x) dx)

content.meaning: let u = x^2 + 1 so that du = 2x dx, which exactly matches the 2x dx already in the integrand

content.label: Choose the substitution variable
content.action: identify the inner function u = x^2 + 1 because its derivative 2x already appears in the integrand
content.purpose: set up a substitution that absorbs the 2x dx factor

content.label: Rewrite the whole integral in u
content.action: rewrite the integral in terms of u: integral 2x(x^2+1)^5 dx becomes integral u^5 du
content.purpose: convert the whole integrand, including dx, into the single variable u

content.label: Integrate in u
content.action: apply the power rule to integral u^5 du to get u^6/6 + C
content.purpose: carry out the now-elementary integral in u

content.label: Return to the original variable
content.action: replace u with x^2 + 1 to express the antiderivative in x: (x^2 + 1)^6/6 + C
content.purpose: return the answer to the original variable x
